CHANGES IN OISD-STD-116

CHANGE IN SCOPE (CLAUSE 2.0) 
This standard also covers the facilities located within the 
refineries and oil/ gas processing plants which includes:
• Petrochemical complexes
Addition of Petrochemical 
complexes

Definition (CLAUSE 3.0) 
• Definition of “Classification of fire” has been 
revised.
• Definition of Oil and gas processing plants has 
been revised.
• Some other new definitions like auto ignition 
temperature, Tank, water spray system, water 
sprinkler system, control room & petrochemical 
complexes have been added.
Definitions
(Clause 3.0)

FIRE PROTECTION PHILOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.1   FIXED WATER SPRAY SYSTEM ON STORAGE TANKS
Class ‘A’ Petroleum in above ground tanks (floating or fixed roof) shall have fixed water spray system, having 
capacity larger than 190 m3 or diameter greater than 6 m. 
4.2.2    SEMI-FIXED FOAM SYSTEM FOR STORAGE TANKS
Fixed roof tanks storing Class 'A' and class 'B' petroleum products, of diameter ≥ 9 m.
4.2.4    AUTOMATIC WATER SPRAY FOR STORAGE AND HANDLING OF LPG /C4 AND LIGHTER ENDS/ 
HYDROGEN 
Automatic Water Spray shall be provided for the following areas:
I.
LPG/C4 and lighter ends and hydrogen, pressure storage vessels shall be provided with automatic water 
spray system.
II.
LPG bottling stations, C4 & Lighter ends - loading or unloading gantries, pumps, and compressors.
III.
Hydrogen compressors associated with storage facilities.

FIRE PROTECTION PHIOLOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.5   WATER SPRAY SYSTEM IN PROCESS UNIT 
I.
Un-insulated vessels having maximum design liquid holdup capacity larger than 50 m3 
containing class A or B flammable liquid in process unit along with offsite area. 
II.
Automatic water spray system for pumps handling LPG or hydrocarbons above auto-
ignition temperature.
III.
Process compressors of C4 or lighter and Hydrogen. 
IV.
Water spray rings for columns of height more than 45 M (above grade level) shall be 
provided. As an alternate, remote operated elevated monitors (at least two, positioned 
diagonally opposite sides) shall be installed at suitable elevation or high-capacity trolley 
mounted monitors shall be available, such that they should cover topmost potential source 
of leakage of column.
Note:- In case there is variance with the recommendations of licensor, then more stringent shall 
be prevailing.

FIRE PROTECTION PHIOLOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.7   WATER SPRINKLER SYSTEM
A water sprinkler system should be provided in polymer extruder buildings, pelletizing building, 
polymer bagging & its warehouse of a petrochemical complex excluding water reactive/sensitive 
material storage area. Car parking in the basement of any refinery or petrochemical complex 
should also be protected with a sprinkler system. For application density & other design aspects 
of any fire sprinkler system, NFPA-13/IS 15105 may be referred.
4.2.8   WATER CURTAIN SYSTEM FOR NAPHTHA & GAS CRACKER FURNACES
The water curtain shall be provided in cases where hydrocarbon gases are being handled in the 
vicinity of the cracker furnace in the process unit. The water curtain shall be actuated using 
Hydrocarbon (HC) detectors placed in the vicinity of the cracker furnace. The water curtain shall 
be designed as per NFPA 15 or equivalent at a design flow rate of 37 lpm/m. The water curtain 
coverage should extend a minimum of 1 m above the flame box of a furnace.

FIRE PROTECTION PHIOLOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.9   CLEAN AGENT FIRE SUPPRESSION SYSTEM FOR CONTROL ROOM, INSTRUMENT 
PANEL ROOM & SATELLITE RACK ROOM (SRR)
Clean Agent System along with all equipment, software for hydraulic calculations and 
corresponding design concentration of clean agent shall be listed or approved by national or 
international standards such as BIS/UL/FM/VdS/LPCB etc. Cylinder along with cylinder valve 
assembly shall have regulatory approval."
4.2.11   DRY CHEMICAL FIRE SUPPRESSION SYSTEM
Water reactive material (e.g. TEAL) handling areas including pumps shall be protected with an  
automatic DCP flooding system. Suitable DCP/metal firefighting DCP shall be used in DCP 
flooding system for TEAL area. DCP flooding system shall be designed as per NFPA-17.

FIRE PROTECTION PHIOLOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.12   FIRE PROTECTION FOR COAL HANDLING AND CONVEYOR SYSTEM 
Refinery/Petchem facilities where Coal based Captive Power plant available for power and steam 
generation. Following Fire protection system shall be installed for the protection of facilities:
Fire Hydrants, wet risers, and Hose reels
Fire water Monitors – For structures having height more than 15 m.
Automatic High Velocity Water spray system for Boiler burner fronts
Manual medium velocity water spray system with proportioner to mix water with suitable UL 
approved wetting agent for Coal silos. 
Water sprinkler system for open type coke/coal conveyor.
Suitable fire detection system.

FIRE PROTECTION PHIOLOSOPHY (CLAUSE 4.0)
4.2.13   FIRE PROTECTION FOR STORAGE / HANDLING OF FLAMMABLE CRYOGENIC LIQUIDS
Cryogenic liquids are liquefied gases that are maintained at very low temperatures (typically less than -
150°C). These liquids are gases at ambient temperatures and pressures. Since these liquids are extremely 
cold and can expand to very large volumes of gases, they are a safety hazard. 
Fire measures required for the same are as below.
• Portable Fire Extinguisher as per Section 12.1
• Suitable type of PPEs 
• Fire water network / Hydrants & Monitors: as per section 5.6.1 & 5.6.2
• Foam system: Portable / fixed High expansion foam generator shall be provided for protection of :
I.
DWST (double walled storage tank) dykes for handling spills. 
II.
Underground (Pit) liquid transfer pumps.

FIRE WATER SYSTEM (CLAUSE 5.0)
5.0   FIRE WATER SYSTEM
Water is a universal fire extinguishing agent and meets most ideal properties required for fire 
extinguishment. The ideal fire extinguishing agent should have the following characteristics: -
a. 
It must be non-flammable
b. 
It should have a high heat of vaporization
c. 
It should be mobile liquid at normal ambient temperature.
d. 
It should be non-toxic and should not decompose to form toxic products. 
Thus, water is the best available option and can be used for fire extinguishment, fire control, 
cooling of equipment and protection of equipment as well as protection of personnel from heat 
radiation. Water should be used such as straight jet, water fog, water mist, water curtain, water 
spray, deluge/ sprinkler, for foam making etc. 
The firewater system shall comprise fire water storage, fire water pumps and distribution piping 
network along with hydrants and monitors, as the main components.

FIRE WATER SYSTEM (CLAUSE 5.0)
5.1   FIRE WATER FLOW RATE
Fire Water flow rate for tank farm shall be aggregated of the following:
a. Water flow calculated for all other tanks situated in the same dyke area a rate of 3 Ipm/m2 of 
tank shell area 
5.2   HEADER PRESSURE
The fire water system shall be designed for a minimum pressure of 7.0 kg/cm2 at the hydraulically 
remotest point of application at grade level and minimum residual pressure of 5.25 kg/cm2 at 
landing valve at elevated structure on designed flow rate at that point. 
5.4.3   STANDBY PUMPS
• In case total number working pumps are up to 2, standby pumps shall be at least one.
• In case number of working pumps are 3 to 4, the number of standby pumps shall be at least 2. 
For more than 4 working pumps, the number of standby pumps shall be at least 3.

FIRE WATER SYSTEM (CLAUSE 5.0)
5.4.4   JOCKEY PUMPS
The capacity of jockey pumps shall be minimum 1% of the design fire water rate. Its head shall be 
higher than the main fire water pumps. Auto cut-in / cut-off facility should be provided for jockey 
pumps.
5.4.5   POWER SUPPLY FOR FIRE WATER PUMPS
The power supply to the electric motor driven pumps should be distributed from a switchboard 
having two separate incomers such that failure of any one incomer source does not disrupt the 
power supply to all the electric motor driven pumps.
5.5.3   PROTECTION FOR UNDERGROUND PIPELINES
The mains shall be provided with protection against soil corrosion by suitable coating/wrapping. 
Where pipes are coming out/going in from/into the ground, the underground anticorrosion 
coating on the pipe will continue for a length of at least 300 mm above ground.

FIRE WATER SYSTEM (CLAUSE 5.0)
5.6.4   FIXED REMOTE / MANUAL OPERATED, MOBILE HIGH-VOLUME LONG-RANGE 
WATER OR WATER CUM FOAM  MONITORS
I.
The number and capacity of monitors shall be provided in such a way that cumulative foam  
application rate from the designated monitors meet the application rate of 9.75 lpm/m2 for 
total burning surface of the affected tank to extinguish the full surface tank fire. Additional 
monitors shall be provided in such a way that each tank is in the coverage area of at least 
two monitors.  Due consideration has been given to compensate for the losses (50%) due to 
wind and other factors.
II.
Monitors should be located to achieve the foam discharge preferably close to tank center 
from upwind direction of tank on fire.
III.
A suitable connections for water supply to portable trailer mounted HVLR should be made 
available.

FIRE WATER SYSTEM (CLAUSE 5.0)
5.6.4   FIXED REMOTE / MANUAL OPERATED, MOBILE HIGH-VOLUME LONG-RANGE 
WATER OR WATER CUM FOAM  MONITORS
The basic water cum foam monitors shall be listed or approved by national or international 
agencies such as BIS, UL and FM etc. or certified by the approved Third-Party Inspection (TPI) 
(only in case of high-volume mobile trailer mounted monitors) agency of the entity. The 
electrical or hydraulic remote-control mechanism shall be conforming to Hazardous Area 
Classification.

FOAM SYSTEM(CLAUSE 6.0)
6.4   FIXED ROOF TANK PROTECTION USING FOAM
Foam conveying system shall have same features as of floating roof tank excepting that a vapour 
seal chamber is required before the foam discharge outlet.
Features of the foam system for fixed roof protection for class A &B shall be as follows:
The vapour seal chamber shall be provided with an effective and durable seal, fragile under low 
pressure (at an inlet pressure of not less than 10 psi (69 kPa) and not greater than 25 psi (172 
kPa), to prevent entrance of vapour into the foam conveying piping system.

FOAM SYSTEM(CLAUSE 6.0)
6.4   FIXED ROOF TANK PROTECTION USING FOAM
Tanks should be provided with foam discharge outlets/ vapour seal chambers as indicated below:
  Tank Diameter in m. 
Minimum number of foam discharge outlet
•  ≥  9 upto 20                              2
•  >20 upto 25                              3
•  >25 upto 30                              4
•  >30 upto 35                              5
•  >35 upto 40                              6
•  >40 upto 45                              8
•  >45 upto 50                             10
                                       Plus 1 outlet for
                                      each additional 465 m2 of area

FOAM SYSTEM(CLAUSE 6.0) =

Amrit Mahotsav

6.6 FULL SURFACE STORAGE TANK FIREFIGHTING & LOGISTICS

New chapter in details on scenario basis.

FOAM SYSTEM (CLAUSE 6.0)
6.8   FOAM APPLICATION RATE
For cone roof tanks containing liquid hydrocarbons, the foam solution delivery rate shall be at least 5 
Ipm/m2 of liquid surface area of the tank to be protected. 
For floating roof tanks containing liquid hydrocarbons, foam solution delivery rate shall be at least 12 
Ipm/m2 of seal area with foam dam height of 600 mm of the tank to be protected. In case of floating roof 
sinking, the rate considered should be 9.75 Ipm/m2 of liquid surface areas for 65 minutes.
DURATION OF FOAM DISCHARGE
The equipment shall be capable of providing primary protection at the specified delivery rates for the 
following minimum period of time.
i)   Tanks containing liquid hydrocarbons - Class 'C' Petroleum             - 30 minutes.
ii)  Tanks containing Class 'A' & 'B' Petroleum or liquids heated            - 65 minutes.
      above their flash points
iii) Where the system's primary purpose is for spill fire protection          - 30 minutes.
Note:- It is advised to keep foam compound stock for extended 30 minutes for full surface tank fire 
scenario.

FOAM SYSTEM (CLAUSE 6.0)
6.9   FOAM QUANTITY REQUIREMENT
The aggregate quantity of foam solution for a single largest tank fire should be calculated as the sum of 
foam quantity requirement in clause 6.8 and total indicated below under items (i) & (ii) for a minimum 
period of 65 minutes. The quantity of foam compound required should be calculated based on 1%, 3% or 
6% concentrate. Necessary changes in the equipment shall be made accordingly. 
6.10   FOAM COMPOUND STORAGE
Foam compound should be stored in containers of suitable capacity, in case of protein, fluoro protein or 
AFFF or AR-AFFF or FFFP. Alcohol Resistant Foam (AR-AFFF) shall be used for handling methanol/ ethanol 
fires. Alcohol Resistant Foam (AR-AFFF) can be used for all type of hydrocarbon fire and polar solvent fire 
effectively. Foam compound can also be stored in overhead storage tank of suitable capacity for quick 
filling of foam tender / nurser during emergency.
Cautionary: Alcohol Resistant Foam (AR-AFFF) is preferred for Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) fire.
Note: Alcohol Resistant Synthetic Fluorine Free Foam (AR-SFFF) are environmentally friendly foam may 
be used.

BATTERY FIRE (CLAUSE 8.0)
8.1   FIRE PROTECTION
For fire protection of Li ion battery, Encapsulator Agent (EA) based on spherical micelle 
technology should be used in line with NFPA 18A & NFPA 855 (latest edition).

MODERNIZATION PLAN FOR FIREFIGHTING EQUIPMENT 
(CLAUSE 11.0)
Turbine Based Water Mist System
Turbine based water mist system is a compact, high flow water/ fog generator. The entire system 
is of modular design which includes turbine system, fuel tank and water/ foam supply. By 
injecting water, in the exhaust stream of the gas turbine a fog is produced. Due to the power of 
turbine, this fog can travel a large distance and can be controlled and influenced by the turbine 
speed, water nozzle configuration and water pressure. The fog stream can be pointed to the 
object (fire, tank, gas cloud) with a joystick which hydraulically controls the angle of the turbine, 
up or down and left or right.
Fire extinguisher of HDPE / Composite material body
This will ensure less weight and claimed to have longer shelf life. This will be more useful in 
corrosion prone area like offshore.

MOBILE FIRE FIGHTING EQUIPMENT (CLAUSE 13.0)
13.1   FIRE TENDERS
The exact number of fire tenders shall be higher of the items (a) or (b):
(a) The quantities firmed up in each case based on two simultaneous major fire taking into consideration 
the size, location of the plant and statutory requirements.
(b) The quantities indicated below.
i) 3 nos. of foam tenders out of which two are for firefighting, one for spill /standby. The foam tender 
should have a foam tank capacity of at least 3000 Liter and the pump capacity of minimum 4000 Ipm at 8.5 
kg/cm2.
ii) DCP tender(s) having aggregate 4000 kg capacity, with Nitrogen as expellant gas. These are required for 
fighting LPG/Gas fire. A monitor should have a variable throw of 15/25/40 kg/sec. The throw of the monitor 
shall be 40 to 50 M for the DCP charge.
Minimum 1 number of Foam Nurser with foam compound tank of minimum 8000-liter capacity, with 
suitable pump for foam transfer. Entity shall ensure the continuous availability of foam nurser.
For Gas Processing Units a minimum of 2 nos. of multipurpose fire tender shall be provided. 
Note:- For small petrochemical follow (a) clause of this section.

MOBILE FIRE FIGHTING EQUIPMENT (CLAUSE 13.0)
13.2.2   FIRE TENDERS
For Gas processing plants, in addition to fixed monitors provided in the tank farm, following   
additional mobile equipment should be provided; namely:- 
(i) Minimum 2 nos. of Trolley mounted water cum foam monitors of capacity of minimum 2000  
GPM (456 m3/hr) with field adjustable variable flow which are listed or approved by national or 
international standards such as BIS, UL and FM etc. Foam induction to the monitor shall be 
possible from a minimum 60 m distance from the monitor. 
(ii) 1 to 2 numbers. of portable monitor of capacity ranging from 1800 to 2250 lpm at discharge 
pressure of 7 kg/cm2. 
15.1.4   TOXIC GAS DETECTION: 
i) Toxic gas detectors shall be installed if the risk is applicable. Toxic gas detectors shall be 
mounted considering the prevailing wind direction and wind speed.
ii) Additionally, Toxic gas sensors shall be installed in HVAC air intake duct of buildings that could 
be affected in the event of flammable or toxic release in the surrounding area.

det Gey Geer Freeney

Oi Industry Safety Directorate
